title: melampyrum pratense subsp. commutatum
intro: Melampyrum pratense subsp. commutatum, also known as green figwort, is a type of plant in the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) that is native to Europe and western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of green figwort include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, green figwort is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, and can also be consumed as a fresh or dried herb.
